Crystal Chitin Leggings  
 

Lore Item
Slot: LEGS
AC: 15
STA: +5 MANA: +35 ENDUR: +35
WT: 4.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except MNK NEC WIZ MAG ENC BST
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Crystal Chitin Leggings
Lore Item
Slot: LEGS
AC: 15
STA: +5 MANA: +35 ENDUR: +35
WT: 4.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL except MNK NEC WIZ MAG ENC BST
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Velious Plate 2
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Stackable:No

Tribute:1284

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-11 23:57:47
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious

Rarity: Common

[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Velketor's Labyrinth
NPC Name
a Frenzied Velium Broodling
The Brood Mother

Zone Name
Velketor's Labyrinth


Uploaded November 5th, 2024 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
Post Comment
OBSOLITE???
QuoteReply
# Mar 31 2003 at 9:41 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592793169098
143 posts
The CC legs were always a mystery to me...
evry singe piece of th Cc set has better stats than the legs.Common if gauntlets has AC 20, STR 10 and 35mana..I was expecting legs to be AC 30 STR 15 STA 10..or soemthign in that range..they arer as hell too...
anyway..it was always was FOR THE LOOK....and its was druid wearable (now this was put into game BEFORE luclin )

Yes the shining crystal plate look was amazing..just liek the old days mithrill for rangers...

ANYWAY..then Luclin comes alive...and Brown Chitin Legs ac 12 wis 5 WITH plate look. Druids cannot even wish for better in that price range...
And one more thing - Luclin grafic put the end to the Velios special texturing..so bye-bye crystal plate....


Today with LOY ability to paint evrything into anyhtign.....well..wahst left of this legs?

memories..memories...

P.S. Dear Verant please review the dem CC legs and put them into SAME stats line as rest of CC armor (right,like they ever listen to anyone)
▲ Page top
I would like a pair.
QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2003 at 6:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760067612
8 posts
I am looking for a pair on Terris Thule. Aenolin in game.
▲ Page top
Niice Look
QuoteReply
# Oct 22 2002 at 3:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591782420003
5 posts

The only reason these sell for inflated prices is look, not stats. I have seen one pair on my server for sale in last month listed at 11k, and guess what , they r still there LOL. I would love to own these, and have plenty of loot if i wanted them. But, 11k is crazy. I can see maybe 6k for look alone. I will say this, they must be a very rare drop cause there is a boat load of Gauntlets, arms and shields. But the boots and legs are very rare. Oh well, some unsuspecting trader will drop these for cheap and i will be there to snatch them up.

5 Stamina? thats going to give a druid a whole what? 10hp at level 60?

15 ac? Yeam, go solo Kormiff Military Leggins or something.

These legs /drumroll /suck

# May 29 2002 at 12:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592254624437
1 post
they are not 10k on D RO, and i have not seen em that high for some time. i bought mine TODAY for 5k, and saw them sell for 4k 2 days ago. they are good pants and the graphic is nice. these will tide my warrior over until i get something better.
▲ Page top
Don't get these
QuoteReply
# Feb 02 2002 at 10:51 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253371083
1 post
Only class that should get these, imho, are druids, for ac and mana. Any of the other classes that can use these are MUCH better off with Sebilite Scale Leggings (16ac, +4 STR, +4 WIS, +4 CHA, +2 svp, +2 svf) So they don't look as good, stats are better...plus, Seb Scale are gonna be cheaper than these. CC costs a fortune =P
Just my 2cp

▲ Page top
RE: Don't get these
QuoteReply
# May 29 2002 at 7:36 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768412570
205 posts
I'm sure I can't speak for all druid out there, but I personaly don't see druids needing AC more than stats, saves, bonus mana and bonus hp. While these are awsome leggings, the only reason I can see a druid wearing a pair of these as opposed to Kommerif Military leggings/Seb Scale leggings/heck even gator scale leggings is for style. High AC items on druids makes as much sense as giving mages haste. While there are technicaly situations where it's useful, you would be "wasting" the potential of this item 95%or more of the time.

If you want a big AC item as a druid, go get a shield of bane warding. Has 25 AC and 20 Wis. Then you can afford to play around with a few slots where you don't need that +5 wis gear any longer.

Meirril, Fennin Ro
▲ Page top
RE: Don't get these
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2002 at 10:41 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591767089005

# May 30 2002 at 1:26 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591766765481

Scholar
70 posts
As a 60 DRU with 255 WIS provided by other pieces in my equipment, these attracted me due to their mana. Even though they may not fit you as well as some of the pieces you listed, they do have a place with other druids in other gear/playing/level situations.

But I'd be lieing through my teeth if I didnt say that the beauty of silver plate on a wood elf wasn't a well-loved perk. =)

RE: Don't get these
QuoteReply
# Apr 30 2002 at 10:32 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592253920662
Scholar
72 posts
I'm a Wood Elf and wearing Seb Scale legs. As a ranger, these are the perfect legs until I can afford something much more expensive. In fact, these are excellent Pally/Cleric/Shamman legs as well because they combine high ac and wisdom, as well as strength and some small saves. The charisma doesn't hurt either. Other classes may want to use something else, but these are much more common and therefore much cheaper. I bought my pair for 250pp. I have not even seen a pair of CC legs for sale.

Edited, Tue Apr 30 23:29:30 2002

Price

# Jul 29 2001 at 8:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764720594
1 post
Can somebody give us an idea on how much these go for instead of arguing about if a rogue should be able to wear it or not?
▲ Page top
RE: Price
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2002 at 1:30 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592759261266
Sage
52 posts
About 1.5k - 5k per piece of armor. I haven't seen anything (except the circlet) under 2k though. I also haven't seen the chestguard or leggings for sale yet so they might go for more. There are always boots and gauntlets for sale on Luclin server; everytime I check I see both. I've never seen them over 3k. The other pieces are more rare, I gather, because I almost never see them up for sale, so their prices would almost definitely be higher.



Edited, Thu Sep 5 14:19:53 2002

Ok Rewbix there is Broodmother in GD, this is The Brood Mother. there is a difference you see =0

She spawns deep in Lower Dogs (kobolds) and also has a nother named spider spawn near, The Brood Master which drops the Crystal Chitin BP/Crystal Chitin Circlet.

Kweas Stormreaver
59 Druid of Tunare

A rogue would wear this for the same reason they wear most of their armor: AC

At least for barbarian rogues 15+ AC legs are hard to get. I believe there are two 16 ac legs from OS most rogues can wear, but not barbarians.

Some might want them for looks was well :)

QuoteReply
# Apr 16 2002 at 9:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761384552
6 posts
It is just that tanks need ac and rogues aren't tanks. Rogues stab things in the back and in order to do this well they need high atk, not ac/sta. Yes ac is nice, but atk is much much more important.

▲ Page top
RE: Rogue
QuoteReply
# Mar 05 2001 at 9:31 AM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
not to mention he said "No Rogue? Yet every other plate wearing class?" Which implies a Rogue to be a plate class which its not considered to be.

For those who do not know.
Plate classes are Clr/Pal/Sk/War/Brd
Chain classes are Rog/Rng/Shm
Leather classes are Druid/Monk
Cloth classes are Wiz/Enc/Nec/Mag

of course as many know there are items that are exceptions to the rule but thats why many of those are "magic" items
▲ Page top
